ai.summary_prompt: Summarize Python as a versatile, high-level programming language and its vast ecosystem of libraries.
ai.theme_prompt: Generate brand colors inspired by Python's blue and yellow snake logo and its clean, readable aesthetic.
ai.page_generation_prompt: Create an ecosystem panel page for Python including package management, popular frameworks like Django and Flask, and data science tools.

description: Python is a high-level, interpreted programming language known for its readability and versatile ecosystem supporting web development, data science, and automation.
longDescription:
Python is a powerful, high-level, interpreted programming language that has become one of the most popular languages in the world. Designed with a focus on code readability and simplicity, Python's syntax allows programmers to express concepts in fewer lines of code than might be possible in languages such as C++ or Java.

The Python ecosystem is vast and diverse, encompassing thousands of libraries and frameworks for nearly every imaginable task. In web development, frameworks like Django and Flask provide robust tools for building scalable applications. In the realm of data science and machine learning, libraries such as NumPy, pandas, Scikit-learn, and TensorFlow have made Python the industry standard. Additionally, Python is widely used for automation, scripting, and scientific computing.

Python follows an open-source development model and is managed by the Python Software Foundation. Its package manager, pip, provides access to the Python Package Index (PyPI), which hosts hundreds of thousands of third-party packages. Python's commitment to simplicity, community-driven development, and a "batteries included" philosophy makes it an ideal choice for beginners and experienced developers alike.
minimumRequirements: Modern CPU, 512 MB RAM, 1 GB disk space
recommendedRequirements: 2 GHz dual-core CPU, 2 GB RAM, 5 GB disk space, Internet connection
features: Strong support for Data Science and AI
name: Python Ecosystem

ai.summary_prompt: Summarize Parrot OS as a security-focused Debian-based distro.
ai.theme_prompt: Generate brand colors taking inspiration from Parrot OS' teal/blue glowing aesthetic.
ai.page_generation_prompt: Create a distro panel page for Parrot OS including versions, screenshots, and ISO/torrent metadata.

description: Parrot OS is a Debian-based distribution for ethical hacking, digital forensics, and cybersecurity.
longDescription:
Parrot OS is a comprehensive security-focused Linux distribution based on Debian Testing. It is designed for cybersecurity professionals, penetration testers, privacy advocates, and researchers who need a robust toolkit for ethical hacking and digital forensics.

It includes a full suite of tools for penetration testing, digital forensics, reverse engineering, malware analysis, cryptography, anonymity, and software development. Available in multiple editions—including Security (full toolset), Home (daily use with privacy focus), and Architect (minimal base for customization)—Parrot OS balances cutting-edge security utilities with a lightweight desktop experience via MATE or KDE.

Parrot supports secure browsing via AnonSurf and Tor integration, sandboxed environments using Firejail, containerized tools, and full disk encryption. It's regularly updated and maintained by the ParrotSec team, backed by a global community of ethical hackers, researchers, and privacy enthusiasts. Parrot also prioritizes system performance and can run efficiently on older hardware while providing cutting-edge security capabilities.
minimumRequirements: 1 GHz CPU, 2 GB RAM, 20 GB disk space, 1024×768 graphics
recommendedRequirements: 2 GHz dual-core CPU, 4 GB RAM, 30 GB SSD, 1366×768 graphics, Internet connection
features: Rolling release based on Debian Testing
name: Parrot OS

ai.summary_prompt: Summarize Ruby as a dynamic, open-source programming language with a focus on simplicity and productivity.
ai.theme_prompt: Generate brand colors inspired by Ruby's red gemstone logo and its elegant, developer-friendly aesthetic.
ai.page_generation_prompt: Create an ecosystem panel page for Ruby including RubyGems, Ruby on Rails framework, and its use in web development and DevOps.

description: Ruby is a dynamic, reflective, object-oriented, general-purpose programming language designed for programmer productivity and simplicity.
longDescription:
Ruby is a language of careful balance. Its creator, Yukihiro "Matz" Matsumoto, blended parts of his favorite languages (Perl, Smalltalk, Eiffel, Ada, and Lisp) to form a new language that balanced functional programming with imperative programming. Ruby is highly portable; it is developed mostly on GNU/Linux, but works on many types of UNIX, macOS, Windows, DOS, BeOS, OS/2, etc.

The Ruby ecosystem is best known for the Ruby on Rails framework, which revolutionized web development by emphasizing convention over configuration. Beyond web development, Ruby is widely used in DevOps (Chef, Puppet), automation, and static site generation (Jekyll). The ecosystem is powered by RubyGems, a package manager that provides a standard format for distributing Ruby programs and libraries.

Ruby's philosophy focuses on making developers happy and productive. It features a natural syntax that is easy to read and write, everything in Ruby is an object, and it supports multiple programming paradigms. The community is known for its friendliness and the "MINSWAN" (Matz is nice and so we are nice) principle.
minimumRequirements: Modern CPU, 256 MB RAM, 500 MB disk space
recommendedRequirements: 1 GHz CPU, 1 GB RAM, 2 GB disk space, Internet connection
name: Ruby Ecosystem

ai.summary_prompt: Summarize Node.js as a cross-platform, open-source JavaScript runtime environment that executes JavaScript code outside a web browser.
ai.theme_prompt: Generate brand colors inspired by Node.js' green hexagonal logo and its modern, asynchronous aesthetic.
ai.page_generation_prompt: Create an ecosystem panel page for Node.js including npm, popular frameworks like Express and NestJS, and its role in full-stack development.

description: Node.js is an open-source, cross-platform JavaScript runtime environment that executes JavaScript code outside a web browser.
longDescription:
Node.js is a cross-platform, open-source JavaScript runtime environment built on Chrome's V8 JavaScript engine. It uses an event-driven, non-blocking I/O model that makes it lightweight and efficient, perfect for data-intensive real-time applications that run across distributed devices.

The Node.js ecosystem is one of the largest in the world, centered around npm (Node Package Manager), which is the world's largest software registry. Node.js has transformed web development by enabling developers to use JavaScript for both client-side and server-side code, fostering the rise of full-stack JavaScript development. Popular frameworks like Express, NestJS, and Fastify have further extended its capabilities for building web servers and APIs.

Node.js is widely used for building web servers, real-time communication tools (like chat applications), microservices architectures, and command-line tools. It is maintained by the OpenJS Foundation and benefits from a massive, active community that continuously contributes to its growth and stability. Node.js' ability to handle many concurrent connections with high performance has made it a favorite among startups and large enterprises alike.
minimumRequirements: Modern CPU, 512 MB RAM, 1 GB disk space
recommendedRequirements: 2 GHz dual-core CPU, 2 GB RAM, 5 GB disk space, Internet connection
features: Very Fast (built on Chrome's V8 engine)
name: Node.js Ecosystem
